title: XmlCompare Configuration
description: JSON Schema for xmlcompare config.json / config.yaml files

tolerance.description: Relative numeric tolerance for fuzzy matching (e.g. 0.001 = 0.1%)

ignore_case.description: Ignore letter case in text comparisons

unordered.description: Treat child element lists as unordered sets

ignore_namespaces.description: Strip XML namespace prefixes and URIs before comparing

skip_keys.description: XPath-style paths of elements/attributes to exclude (e.g. //timestamp)

skip_pattern.description: Regex pattern; elements whose tag matches are excluded

filter_xpath.description: Only compare elements matching this XPath expression

output_file.description: Write report to this file path instead of stdout

summary.description: Show summary counts only, not full difference list

verbose.description: Emit detailed comparison trace to stderr

quiet.description: Suppress all output; rely on exit code only

fail_fast.description: Stop at the first detected difference

structure_only.description: Compare element structure only; ignore text content and attributes

max_depth.description: Maximum element nesting depth to compare

schema.description: Path to XSD schema file for pre-validation and type hints

type_aware.description: Use schema type hints for smarter date/numeric comparison

plugins.description: Plugin module paths or class names to load

stream.description: Use streaming (SAX) parser for memory-efficient large file comparison

parallel.description: Compare directory file pairs in parallel

threads.description: Worker thread count (0 = auto-detect from CPU cores)

match_attr.description: Attribute name used as match key for --unordered repeated elements (e.g. 'id')

diff_only.description: Suppress output for equal file pairs in --dirs mode

canonicalize.description: Strip XML comment nodes and processing instructions before comparing

xslt.description: Path to XSLT 1.0 stylesheet applied to both documents before comparison

cache_file.description: Path to incremental cache file for --dirs mode (skips unchanged equal pairs)

swap.description: Swap file1 and file2 before comparing (reverse expected/actual direction)

no_color.description: Disable ANSI color output regardless of TTY detection
